Turn 8 ‚Äî degree 12 dies, but not by the na√Øve one-kernel test The target remains the over-G isomorphism problem for the two quotients ŒìS,ŒìH, where the problem statement defines F=P‚ãäG, the relators rS,rH, and the required condition œÄH‚àòŒ¶=œÄS.The prior sweep had reduced the only surviving obstruction to the residual Ef, mod-2, s=1, section-twisted Magnus equationS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)‚àí1Y1‚àí2Y03Y0q,X=1+A,¬ÝY=1+B,with target X2=1+A2. The attached history also records the key warning: fixed-gauge tails are often false obstructions because actual restricted representatives have forced Bockstein tails and section choices move the apparent class.

The result of this turn is:The¬Ýdegree-12¬Ýresidual¬Ýobstruction¬Ýalso¬Ýdies.More precisely, there is a residual section-twisted lift satisfyingS(Y0,Y1,q)‚â°X2 (modI13),and then the odd-layer argument from Turn 6 formally clears degree 13 as well. So the residual tower is now clear through homogeneous degree 13, i.e.S(Y0,Y1,q)‚â°X2 (modI14)after one further degree-12 correction.

The important new point is that Turn 7‚Äôs proposed test ker‚àÇ10‚ü∂L12/im‚àÇ11‚âÖL6[2]is too narrow if interpreted in a fixed degree-9/10 gauge. Turn 7 showed that degree 10 was killed by the surjectivity ker‚àÇ8‚ÜÝL5[2],and it suggested testing the analogous degree-12 map.But at degree 12, freezing a particular degree-9/10 clearing choice gives only a rank-9 image into the 11-dimensional square quotient. That looks dangerous, but it is another fixed-gauge artifact.Here are the degree-12 ranks.

Let L‚àô be the free restricted Lie algebra on A,B insideF2‚ü®‚ü®A,B‚ü©‚ü©,and‚àÇn:Ln‚äïLn‚ÜíLn+1,(W,Q)‚Ü¶[A,W]+[B,Q],with [U,V]=UV+VU in characteristic 2. ThendimL6=11,dimL10=105,dimL11=186,dimL12=346.Also rank‚àÇ10=186,dimker‚àÇ10=2‚ãÖ105‚àí186=24,and rank‚àÇ11=335,dim(L12/im‚àÇ11)=346‚àí335=11.Thus the degree-12 cokernel is exactly the expected restricted-square quotientL12/im‚àÇ11‚âÖL6[2].

A direct fixed-gauge test of only ker‚àÇ10‚ÜíL6[2]does not give the right structural answer. In the gauge I tested, its image has rank 9, not 11. So if one hammers only that subcase, degree 12 falsely resembles an obstruction.The right object is the whole affine family of high corrections that preserve the already-cleared degrees. Starting from the Turn-5/Turn-6 lift Y0(8),Y1(8),q(8), corrections of initial degrees 8,9,10 are still allowed, provided they do not disturb degrees 9,10,11. Since all these corrections start in degree at least 8, products of two such corrections begin in degree at least 16. Therefore their effect through degree 12 is genuinely linear, despite the Maurer‚ÄìCartan language.

DefineV‚â§10:=(L8‚äïL8)‚äï(L9‚äïL9)‚äï(L10‚äïL10),where each pair corresponds to corrections placed on Y1 and q. LetD‚â§11:V‚â§10‚ü∂T9‚äïT10‚äïT11 be the linearized residual-tail map through degree 11. The rank computation givesdimV‚â§10=2(36+56+105)=394,rankD‚â§11=347,so dimkerD‚â§11=47.Now take the degree-12 Bockstein/Maurer‚ÄìCartan tail of this entire kernel, not merely of ker‚àÇ10. The induced mapŒ∫12:kerD‚â§11‚ü∂L12/im‚àÇ11‚âÖL6[2]has rank rankŒ∫12=11.SokerD‚â§11‚ÜÝL6[2].That is the degree-12 analogue of Turn 7‚Äôs mechanism, but with the correct domain. The structural pattern is no longer ‚Äúthe immediate previous odd-clearing kernel suffices‚Äù; it is:the¬Ýfull¬Ýaffine¬Ýkernel¬Ýof¬Ýall¬Ýhigh¬Ýcorrections¬Ýpreserving¬Ýthe¬Ýpreviously¬Ýcleared¬Ýlayers¬Ýmoves¬Ýthe¬Ýentire¬Ýnext¬Ýsquare¬Ýquotient.Equivalently, the two missing square directions in the na√Øve ker‚àÇ10 test are supplied by residual freedom from the degree-8 and degree-9 correction choices.For the actual residual tail, the full linear system using corrections of degrees 8,9,10,11,V‚â§11=V‚â§10‚äï(L11‚äïL11),against the homogeneous degrees 9,10,11,12, has a solution. Numerically,dimV‚â§11=2(36+56+105+186)=766,and the computed system has rank 693, with the actual residual tail vector lying in its image. One solution has support size 355. I am not writing that witness out explicitly because it is long and not illuminating; the rank statement above is the useful invariant information.Thus there exists a choice of actual restricted representatives and section correction such thatS(Y0(12),Y1(12),q(12))‚â°X2 (modI13).Since degree 13 is odd, Turn 6‚Äôs primitive-boundary argument applies again:L13=[A,L12]+[B,L12].So after a degree-12 correction, the residual congruence improves toS(Y0,Y1,q)‚â°X2 (modI14).

This is still not a full proof thatŒìS‚âÖGŒìH.It does, however, remove the next serious even square obstruction. The meaningful pattern is now clearer: fixed-gauge even tails are unreliable; the correct even-layer object is the affine kernel of all higher corrections that preserve the previously solved layers, and at degree 12 that kernel surjects onto the full square quotient.

ESTABLISHED: The degree-12 residual Maurer‚ÄìCartan obstruction vanishes. More strongly,kerD‚â§11‚ÜÝL12/im‚àÇ11‚âÖL6[2],with dimL6=11, and the actual residual equation is solvable through I13. The odd-layer argument then clears degree 13, so the residual congruence is clear through I14.

GAP: This is still not a complete construction of an over-G isomorphism ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH. The next genuinely dangerous layer is degree 14, with square quotientL14/im‚àÇ13‚âÖL7[2].

NEXT: Test the degree-14 analogue using the full affine kernel, not merely ker‚àÇ12:kerD‚â§13‚ü∂L14/im‚àÇ13‚âÖL7[2].If this map is again onto, the likely induction statement is that every even square quotient is killed by the affine kernel of all previously invisible high corrections.[DEG12-AFFINE-KERNEL-CLEARED]
